scene_text: INT. PARKING GARAGE - NIGHT. Fluorescent lights flicker overhead, casting stuttering shadows across rows of empty cars. DETECTIVE SARA COLE (40s, trench coat, exhausted eyes) moves low between vehicles, gun drawn. Her breath fogs in the cold air. Footsteps echo somewhere behind her — getting closer. She presses her back against a concrete pillar, listening. A car alarm BLARES suddenly. She spins. Nothing. Then a hand grabs her shoulder from behind.

The space feels vast and empty — a concrete labyrinth.", "camera_angle": "wide shot", "mood": "tense", "lighting": "harsh shadows", "characters_present": [], "narrator_line": "Three levels underground, the city above feels like another world entirely.", "music_recommendation": "low drone with irregular percussion hits"}, {"beat_number": 2, "visual_description": "Medium shot of Sara moving in a combat crouch between two cars, gun raised in both hands. Her trench coat drags silently. Her eyes scan every shadow.", "camera_angle": "medium shot", "mood": "tense", "lighting": "low-key dramatic", "characters_present": ["SARA"], "narrator_line": "She had been a detective long enough to know when she was being hunted."}, {"beat_number": 3, "visual_description": "Extreme close-up of Sara's ear as she tilts her head, listening. The faint echo of footsteps reverberates in the background.", "camera_angle": "extreme close-up", "mood": "tense", "lighting": "low-key dramatic", "characters_present": ["SARA"], "narrator_line": "Every sound in this place carried — and something was getting closer."}, {"beat_number": 4, "visual_description": "Low angle shot looking up at Sara pressing her back flat against a concrete pillar, gun pointed at the ceiling, jaw tight.", "camera_angle": "low angle", "mood": "tense", "lighting": "harsh shadows", "characters_present": ["SARA"], "narrator_line": "She made herself small. She made herself still. She waited."}, {"beat_number": 5, "visual_description": "Wide shot of the garage as a car alarm suddenly BLARES — red hazard lights pulsing across the entire space. Sara spins toward it, gun leveled.", "camera_angle": "wide shot", "mood": "tense", "lighting": "low-key dramatic", "characters_present": ["SARA"], "narrator_line": "The alarm split the silence like a scream."}, {"beat_number": 6, "visual_description": "Over-the-shoulder shot from behind Sara as she scans the direction of the alarm — nothing there. Just blinking hazard lights and empty cars.", "camera_angle": "over-the-shoulder", "mood": "mysterious", "lighting": "harsh shadows", "characters_present": ["SARA"], "narrator_line": "Nothing. A decoy, or her nerves finally breaking."}, {"beat_number": 7, "visual_description": "Extreme close-up of a hand slamming down on Sara's shoulder from behind, fingers gripping tight. Her eyes go wide.", "camera_angle": "extreme close-up", "mood": "tense", "lighting": "low-key dramatic", "characters_present": ["SARA"], "narrator_line": "It wasn't her nerves."}]

scene_text: EXT. FARMHOUSE - DUSK. A rusted pickup truck sits in tall grass. The farmhouse beyond it is dark — every window black, front door hanging open at an angle. MARCUS (20s, backpack, phone flashlight on) steps out of his car and stares at it. His friend JESS (20s) appears beside him, arms crossed. Neither speaks. A crow lands on the porch railing and stares at them. From somewhere inside the house comes a slow, rhythmic KNOCKING. Marcus takes a step forward.
beat_breakdown: [{"beat_number": 1, "visual_description": "Wide establishing shot of the farmhouse at dusk. A rusted pickup sits half-swallowed by tall grass in the foreground. The house looms behind it — dark windows, door hanging open. The sky is bruised purple and orange.", "camera_angle": "wide shot", "mood": "dark", "lighting": "golden hour", "characters_present": [], "narrator_line": "The house had been empty for eleven years. Or so the county records said.", "music_recommendation": "sparse piano notes with long reverb tail"}, {"beat_number": 2, "visual_description": "Medium shot of Marcus and Jess standing side by side in front of their car, staring at the farmhouse. Neither moves. The phone flashlight in Marcus's hand seems very small against the dark.", "camera_angle": "medium shot", "mood": "dark", "lighting": "golden hour", "characters_present": ["MARCUS", "JESS"], "narrator_line": "They had driven four hours to get here. Neither of them wanted to be the first to say turn back."}, {"beat_number": 3, "visual_description": "Close-up of the farmhouse front door — hanging open at a crooked angle, darkness beyond it absolute. The wood is warped and grey.", "camera_angle": "close-up", "mood": "mysterious", "lighting": "low-key dramatic", "characters_present": [], "narrator_line": "The door had been open a long time. Whatever left hadn't bothered to close it."}, {"beat_number": 4, "visual_description": "Close-up of a crow landing on the porch railing with a sharp flap of wings. It turns its head and stares directly at camera — at Marcus and Jess.", "camera_angle": "close-up", "mood": "dark", "lighting": "golden hour", "characters_present": [], "narrator_line": "The crow didn't startle. It watched them the way things watch when they already know how it ends."}, {"beat_number": 5, "visual_description": "Extreme close-up of Marcus's ear and the side of his face as he tilts his head, listening. His expression shifts — he hears something.", "camera_angle": "extreme close-up", "mood": "tense", "lighting": "low-key dramatic", "characters_present": ["MARCUS"], "narrator_line": "Then they both heard it. Slow. Rhythmic. From somewhere deep inside."}, {"beat_number": 6, "visual_description": "Low angle shot from the grass looking up at Marcus as he takes one step forward toward the house, phone flashlight cutting a thin beam ahead of him.", "camera_angle": "low angle", "mood": "dark", "lighting": "low-key dramatic", "characters_present": ["MARCUS"], "narrator_line": "He told himself it was pipes. Old houses knock. He told himself a lot of things."}]

scene_text: INT. LATE-NIGHT DINER - 2AM. The place is almost empty — a cook visible through the pass-through, a tired waitress refilling sugar dispensers at the far end. ELENA (30s, paint-stained jacket, hair escaping a bun) sits alone in a corner booth, sketchbook open, coffee going cold. The bell above the door JINGLES. DANIEL (30s, soaked from rain, tie loosened) comes in and scans the room. Every booth is taken except one — the one across from Elena. He approaches. She doesn't look up. He slides in across from her. A beat. She looks up.
beat_breakdown: [{"beat_number": 1, "visual_description": "Wide shot of the diner interior at 2AM. Warm amber light, cracked vinyl booths, a nearly empty room. A cook moves behind the pass-through. The whole place feels suspended in time.", "camera_angle": "wide shot", "mood": "melancholic", "lighting": "candlelit", "characters_present": [], "narrator_line": "At 2AM the city empties out, and the people left awake find each other.", "music_recommendation": "slow jazz guitar, single instrument, intimate"}, {"beat_number": 2, "visual_description": "Medium shot of Elena alone in a corner booth, sketchbook open in front of her, pencil moving. Paint stains on her jacket sleeve. Coffee cup pushed to the side, forgotten.", "camera_angle": "medium shot", "mood": "melancholic", "lighting": "candlelit", "characters_present": ["ELENA"], "narrator_line": "She came here when the apartment felt too quiet. The sketches kept her company."}, {"beat_number": 3, "visual_description": "Close-up of Elena's sketchbook — a half-finished drawing of a face, expressive and unfinished. Her pencil pauses mid-stroke.", "camera_angle": "close-up", "mood": "melancholic", "lighting": "candlelit", "characters_present": ["ELENA"], "narrator_line": "She never finished the faces. She wasn't sure why."}, {"beat_number": 4, "visual_description": "Wide shot from Elena's POV as the diner door swings open and Daniel enters, rain-soaked, scanning the room. He looks out of place — overdressed, underprepared for the weather.", "camera_angle": "POV shot", "mood": "hopeful", "lighting": "candlelit", "characters_present": ["DANIEL"], "narrator_line": "The door opened. She didn't look up. But she noticed."}, {"beat_number": 5, "visual_description": "Over-the-shoulder shot from behind Daniel as he approaches Elena's booth — the only empty seat. She still hasn't looked up from her sketchbook.", "camera_angle": "over-the-shoulder", "mood": "hopeful", "lighting": "candlelit", "characters_present": ["DANIEL", "ELENA"], "narrator_line": "He had no reason to sit there. Every reason to keep walking."}, {"beat_number": 6, "visual_description": "Medium shot of Daniel sliding into the booth across from Elena. He sets his wet jacket on the seat beside him. A beat of silence.", "camera_angle": "medium shot", "mood": "hopeful", "lighting": "candlelit", "characters_present": ["DANIEL", "ELENA"], "narrator_line": "He sat down like he'd been expected. She didn't tell him he hadn't been."}, {"beat_number": 7, "visual_description": "Close-up of Elena's pencil stopping mid-stroke on the page. And the face she'd never been able to finish suddenly made sense."}]

scene_text: EXT. ROOFTOP - DAY. Blazing sun. KAI (30s, athletic, earpiece in) sprints across a rooftop, leaping a ventilation shaft without breaking stride. Below, the city is a blur of traffic and noise. He glances back — two PURSUERS in black are closing the gap. Ahead: a six-foot gap between this building and the next. No ledge. No rope. Just air. He doesn't slow down. He hits the edge and launches himself across. For a moment he is entirely airborne. Then his hands catch the far ledge. He pulls himself up and keeps running.
beat_breakdown: [{"beat_number": 1, "visual_description": "Wide shot of the rooftop from above — a vast expanse of gravel and HVAC units under a blazing midday sun. A tiny figure (Kai) sprints across it at full speed.", "camera_angle": "high angle", "mood": "action", "lighting": "natural daylight", "characters_present": ["KAI"], "narrator_line": "Forty stories up, the rules of the street didn't apply. Up here it was just speed and nerve.", "music_recommendation": "driving electronic percussion, fast tempo, no melody"}, {"beat_number": 2, "visual_description": "Tracking shot running alongside Kai at eye level as he leaps a ventilation shaft mid-stride without slowing. His face is focused, jaw set — this is not his first rooftop.", "camera_angle": "tracking shot", "mood": "action", "lighting": "natural daylight", "characters_present": ["KAI"], "narrator_line": "He had mapped this route three weeks ago. He hoped he'd remembered it right."}, {"beat_number": 3, "visual_description": "Over-the-shoulder shot from behind Kai as he glances back mid-sprint. Two figures in black are visible far behind him, gaining ground.", "camera_angle": "over-the-shoulder", "mood": "tense", "lighting": "natural daylight", "characters_present": ["KAI", "PURSUERS"], "narrator_line": "They were faster than he'd expected. They always were."}, {"beat_number": 4, "visual_description": "Wide shot of the rooftop edge approaching fast — and beyond it, a six-foot gap of open air, the next building's rooftop slightly lower. No bridge. No rope.", "camera_angle": "wide shot", "mood": "tense", "lighting": "natural daylight", "characters_present": ["KAI"], "narrator_line": "The gap was six feet. He'd cleared worse. He told himself that."}, {"beat_number": 5, "visual_description": "Low angle shot from below the rooftop edge looking up as Kai hits the lip and launches — body fully extended, suspended against a white-hot sky.", "camera_angle": "low angle", "mood": "action", "lighting": "natural daylight", "characters_present": ["KAI"], "narrator_line": "For one second he was weightless. For one second anything was possible."}, {"beat_number": 6, "visual_description": "Close-up of Kai's hands slamming down onto the far ledge, fingers gripping concrete, knuckles white. The rest of him followed."}, {"beat_number": 7, "visual_description": "Wide tracking shot as Kai hauls himself up onto the far rooftop and immediately breaks back into a sprint without pausing, disappearing between HVAC units.", "camera_angle": "tracking shot", "mood": "action", "lighting": "natural daylight", "characters_present": ["KAI"], "narrator_line": "He didn't stop to celebrate. scene_text: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - EVENING. The room is quiet except for the soft beep of monitors. RUTH (70s, small in the hospital bed, eyes open) watches the window where the last light is fading. Her son MICHAEL (40s, still in work clothes, tie loosened) sits in a plastic chair beside her, leaning forward with his elbows on his knees. He's been here for hours. Neither has spoken in a long time. Ruth slowly reaches out her hand. Michael looks down at it. Then he takes it. Outside the window, the city lights begin to come on, one by one.
